questions.question_text: Under Chapter 709, within how many days of acceptance must the seller provide the Real Estate Condition Report?
distractors: 5 days
15 days
30 days
questions.explanation: Wisconsin law requires sellers to furnish a completed Real Estate Condition Report to prospective buyers no later than 10 days after acceptance of the contract.

questions.question_text: What standard of disclosure applies to the Wisconsin Real Estate Condition Report?
questions.correct_answer: Sellers disclose what they actually know
distractors: Sellers must hire an inspector before disclosing
Sellers must investigate all possible defects
Sellers can choose what to disclose
questions.explanation: Wisconsin uses a 'known' standard — sellers disclose what they actually know about the property's condition. They have no duty to inspect or investigate, but must be honest and disclose in good faith.

questions.question_text: Which areas does the Wisconsin Real Estate Condition Report cover?
distractors: Only structural defects visible to the eye
Only environmental contamination issues
Only issues found during a professional inspection
questions.explanation: The Condition Report covers structural defects (roof, foundation, HVAC, plumbing), environmental issues (wells, asbestos, lead, radon, tanks), and legal issues (boundary disputes, shared wells, encumbrances).

questions.question_text: A seller knows the basement floods during heavy rain but does not disclose this. What obligation was violated?
questions.correct_answer: The duty to provide honest, good-faith disclosure
distractors: The duty to repair all known defects first
The duty to hire a professional inspector
No obligation, since flooding is weather-related
questions.explanation: The Condition Report requires honest, good-faith disclosure of known conditions. Knowingly failing to disclose a recurring basement flooding problem violates this requirement.

questions.question_text: A seller inherited a property and never lived there. Must they still provide a Condition Report?
questions.correct_answer: No, sellers who never occupied are exempt
distractors: Yes, all residential sellers must provide one
Yes, but only for properties over $200,000
No, but only if the property is being sold as-is
questions.explanation: The Condition Report has several exemptions, including sellers who never occupied the property. Since they never lived there, they may not have knowledge of the property's condition.

questions.question_text: Which property type is exempt from the Wisconsin Condition Report requirement?
questions.correct_answer: Multi-unit properties with 5 or more units
distractors: Any property built before 1978
Properties listed for less than $100,000
Commercial properties converted to residential
questions.explanation: Properties with 5 or more units are exempt from the Condition Report. Other exemptions include estates/trusts, foreclosure sales, new construction (builder warranty applies), and sellers who never occupied.

questions.question_text: A seller fails to provide the Condition Report within 10 days. What can the buyer do?
questions.correct_answer: Rescind within 2 business days after the period expires
distractors: Rescind at any time before closing
Demand a $5,000 penalty from the seller
Nothing, the contract remains fully binding
questions.explanation: If the seller doesn't provide the Condition Report within 10 days, the buyer may rescind within 2 business days after that 10-day period expires. Once the report is received and accepted, rescission rights end.

questions.question_text: A foreclosure property is being sold. Is a Condition Report required?
distractors: Yes, the lender must provide one
Yes, but only for owner-occupied foreclosures
No, unless the buyer specifically requests one
questions.explanation: Foreclosure sales are exempt from the Condition Report requirement. The lender typically has limited knowledge of the property's condition, so the exemption applies.

questions.question_text: New construction is exempt from the Condition Report because:
questions.correct_answer: Builder warranty applies to new homes
distractors: New homes have no possible defects
The buyer inspects before the final walkthrough
DSPS inspects all new construction
questions.explanation: New construction is exempt because the builder warranty serves a similar purpose, providing protection against defects. The builder is responsible for construction quality under their warranty.

questions.question_text: Once a buyer receives and accepts the Condition Report, can they still rescind based on the report?
questions.correct_answer: No, rescission rights end once the report is accepted
distractors: Yes, within 5 business days of acceptance
Yes, if an inspector finds additional defects
Yes, at any time before closing
questions.explanation: The buyer's rescission right exists only if the report is not provided within 10 days. Once the buyer receives and accepts the report, there is no rescission right based on the report itself.

questions.question_text: Who is responsible for cleanup if an underground storage tank on a property leaks?
distractors: The company that installed the tank
The Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources
The previous owner who used the tank
questions.explanation: In Wisconsin, the property owner of record is liable for contamination and remediation of underground storage tanks. USTs must be registered with the WI DNR and properly closed or removed by certified professionals.

questions.question_text: Underground storage tanks in Wisconsin must be registered with which agency?
questions.correct_answer: The Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources
distractors: The Department of Safety and Professional Services
The Real Estate Examining Board
The county Register of Deeds
questions.explanation: USTs must be registered with the Wisconsin DNR. The property owner of record is liable for contamination and remediation. Tanks must be properly closed or removed by certified professionals.

questions.question_text: How often must Private Onsite Wastewater Treatment Systems (POWTS) be inspected?
distractors: Annually
Every 5 years
Only when the property is sold
questions.explanation: POWTS (septic systems) require a visual inspection by a licensed POWTS professional at least every 3 years. Pumping is required when sludge or scum exceeds one-third of the tank volume.

questions.question_text: A property borders a lake in Wisconsin. Development is typically restricted within:
distractors: 100 feet of the lake shore
300 feet of the lake shore
500 feet of the lake shore
questions.explanation: Wisconsin DNR has shoreland zoning jurisdiction. Development restrictions apply within 300 feet of navigable waterways and 1,000 feet of lakes. Wetland delineation may also be required.

questions.question_text: Radon testing in Wisconsin is most commonly performed at what level of the home?
questions.correct_answer: The lowest living level, typically the basement
distractors: The attic, where radon accumulates
The main floor living area only
Outside the home near the foundation
questions.explanation: Radon testing is recommended at the lowest living level, typically the basement in Wisconsin homes. Southern Wisconsin counties tend to have higher radon levels.

questions.question_text: When selling a condominium in Wisconsin, what additional disclosure must be provided?
questions.correct_answer: Association documents, budget, and reserve fund information
distractors: Only the monthly assessment amount
A building inspection report from the HOA
The original developer's marketing materials
questions.explanation: Wisconsin has specific condominium disclosure requirements. Buyers must receive association documents, financial information including the budget and reserve fund, and other relevant association information.

questions.question_text: Federal lead-based paint disclosure is required for homes built before what year?
questions.explanation: Federal law requires lead-based paint disclosure for all residential properties built before 1978, when lead paint was banned for residential use. Sellers must provide known information about lead paint and give buyers a 10-day testing opportunity.

questions.question_text: A listing for a condominium unit built in 1975 would require which disclosures?
questions.correct_answer: Both condominium and lead paint disclosures
distractors: Only the standard Condition Report
Only the lead paint disclosure
Only the condominium disclosure
questions.explanation: A condo built in 1975 triggers both condominium-specific disclosures (association documents, budget) and federal lead paint disclosure (built before 1978). The Condition Report may also apply.

questions.question_text: Which form is used for a condominium listing in Wisconsin?
distractors: WB-1
WB-3
WB-5
questions.explanation: WB-4 is the Residential Condominium Listing Contract. There is also a corresponding WB-14 Residential Condominium Offer to Purchase for the transaction.

questions.question_text: A buyer of a pre-1978 home must be given how many days to conduct a lead paint inspection?
distractors: 5 days
14 days
30 days
questions.explanation: Under federal lead paint disclosure rules, buyers of pre-1978 homes must be given a 10-day opportunity to conduct a lead paint inspection. The buyer may waive this right.
